killMS.Plot.GiveNXNYPanels.GiveNXNYPanels(Ns, ratio=1.6)[source]¶

Function that splits NS into NX,NY values needed to fit the requested ratio

Return type:

(<class ‘int’>, <class ‘int’>)

class killMS.Plot.GiveRectSubplot.giveRectSubPlot(nx, ny, fig=None, pos=(0.1, 0.1, 0.9, 0.9), xylabel=(False, False))[source]¶

Bases: object

giveRect(ii)[source]¶

Returns positions and widths of the rectangle associated with subplot ii

Return type:

tuple

give_axis(i)[source]¶

Returns an Axes object added to self.fig, with desired tick behaviour defined here.

class killMS.Plot.Graph.ClassMplWidget(Ntot, nx=None, ny=None, TBar=True, NPlots=None, SaveName='Fig', **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: object

Define self.mplwidget, with specific subplot, axes properties, patch facecolor defines self.CoordMachine as a giveRectSubPlot return, set ShowTicks to True

class ClassStrValPixel(Dico)[source]¶

Bases: object

Get string-formated value of individual pixel

StrValPixel(x, y)[source]¶

Returns string to print value of data at x,y

Return type:

str

toPix(x0, x1, xi, N)[source]¶

Returns relative position of xi along x0-x1 as an int

Return type:

int

addToolBar()[source]¶

define self.navi_toolbar, add it to self.gridLayout_2

Return type:

None

cla()[source]¶

update canvas, update currentIIm and currentIPlot

Return type:

None

draw()[source]¶

update canvas, flush_events

Return type:

None

imshow(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

if no args provided, set them to a 200x200 random field if self.currentIm set to -1, initialise empty array in self.DicoAxis, and populate it as though it was previously present; then, pylab.draw() if data==None specifically, set it to 200x200 randn, then update self.DicoAxis if currentIm is the last im, set it to -, reiterate ThisPlot.set_data, self.update

Return type:

None

plot(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

if args are empty, set x,y to [0],[0]; yminx,ymax,xmin,xmax to -3,3, args to (x,y) otherwise, read x,y from args[0,1] read xmin,xmax, ymin,ymax from x,y; set limts from minmax if the currentIAx NLines is 0 or currentIPlot > NLines, iterate NLines, assing currentIAx x,y,lims. account for empty dictionary fields end with the append of a plot to self.DicoAxis

Return type:

None

savefig()[source]¶

savefig to self.SaveName_self.CounterSave.png

Return type:

None

setSaveName(name)[source]¶

Define self.SaveName variable

Return type:

None

set_clim(axis, vmin, vmax)[source]¶

read vmin vmax from thisplot im, set_clim to it, write lims to self.dixoaxis, update plot

Return type:

None

set_extent(axis, extent)[source]¶

set_extent input extent to ThisPlot on input axis

Return type:

None

set_title(txt)[source]¶

set title on dicoaxis currentiax ax, then draw

Return type:

None

set_xylims(xlim, ylim)[source]¶

Set xlim, ylim on self.DicoAxis[self.CurrentIAx][“ax”], then draw

Return type:

None

subplot(iAx, share=-1, draw=True, **kwargs)[source]¶

check for iAx in self.DicoAxis.keys(); add it if not yet present set currentiAx, currentAx, Im, IPlot

Return type:

None

text(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

if args is not an array, set x,y to 0 and txt to “” otherwise, read x,y,txt from args 0,1,2 if text is not already in self.DicoAxis[self.CurrentIAx].keys: add it ThisPlot = self.DicoAxis…[“Text”] self.update(ThisPlot)

Return type:

None

update(ThisPlot)[source]¶

redraw CurrentAx

Return type:

None

updateThread(ThisPlot, data)[source]¶

idk where AThread is defined. This sets and starts it.

Return type:

None

updateThread2(ThisPlot, data)[source]¶

idk where GenericThread is defined. looks like it dispatches the artist updates in parallel

Return type:

None

killMS.Plot.Graph.test()[source]¶

Functional test for the plotter
